title: [NO TESTS NEEDED] Podman machine CLI and interface stub
body:
Podman machine will be a mac-only command that manages the VM where
containers are run. Currently, only the CLI is written and the interface
function for the VM management is stub for future developement

The podman machine cli is only built on mac builds.

Signed-off-by: Ashley Cui <acui@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
A few things:

1. Currently the interface functions only print a "Created" or "Started" or "Stopped" 
2. No tests because there's no actual functionality other than printing stuff

Let me know if I should add/change/test anything

@baude @jwhonce PTAL

Man pages...
I would get this passing.  Perhaps add a  simple test to just make sure you get back the errors and all of the options pass.
CI iis not liking this because `machine` doesn't show up on `podman --help` on fedora because its a mac-only command...
@ashley-cui  Let's make it available for Linux as well for now.  We are a ways from merging this, and it might be handy for linux.
A number of scattered nits, otherwise LGTM
